channel.title: The Grant Desk - Visual & Media Arts, Worldwide, added in last 30 days
channel.description: Paid open calls, fellowships and residencies in AI, tech, research, and digital and mixed-media arts, sorted into the right pile. Updated as new calls land on the desk. Filtered to: Visual & Media Arts, Worldwide, added in last 30 days.

item.title: Future Generation Art Prize 2027 (PinchukArtCentre)
item.description:
Organisation: Victor Pinchuk Foundation (PinchukArtCentre)

Location: Kyiv, Ukraine (exhibition of shortlisted artists at PinchukArtCentre in Spring 2027)

Award: Main prize US$100,000 ($60,000 cash + $40,000 investment in the winner's practice). Up to 5 Special Prizes share a total amount of US$20,000 for supporting projects that develop their artistic practice. No application fee.

Duration: Biannual prize cycle (shortlist Sept 2026; exhibition and award ceremony Spring 2027)

Deadline: 28 Jun 2026

Biannual global contemporary art prize from the Victor Pinchuk Foundation (PinchukArtCentre, Kyiv), recognising and supporting a future generation of artists. The main winner receives US$100,000 ($60,000 cash plus $40,000 invested in their practice); up to five special prizes share a total of US$20,000 for projects developing the artist's practice. Shortlisted artists exhibit at PinchukArtCentre in Spring 2027, with the award ceremony following. ELIGIBILITY: open to all artists aged 35 or younger worldwide, with no restrictions on gender, nationality, race or artistic medium; former Prize winners are not eligible to enter again but other previous applicants may re-apply. APPLY through the open call online. A selection committee reviews applications and selects up to twenty artists for the exhibition; in addition, 300 correspondent art experts worldwide nominate two to five candidates each. Applications accepted 11 May - 28 June 2026; shortlist announced 14 September 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Biafarin: BOAPS - Summer 2026 (international online art prize)
item.description:
Organisation: Biafarin

Location: Online (Canadian organisation; worldwide submissions and online exhibition)

Award: Cash grants per artist: Major Grant CAD 500 (1 artist); Merit Grant CAD 250 each (4 artists, total CAD 1,000 pool); Recognition Grant CAD 100 each (10 artists, total CAD 1,000 pool). NOTE: submission is free, but SELECTED ARTISTS PAY a USD 50 selection fee (USD 40 early bird) to participate in the exhibition. Money-back guarantee on the selection fee until exhibition opening day.

Duration: Online group exhibition 1 Jul - 30 Sep 2026 (3 months); up to 10 selected for separate 2-month online solo exhibitions

Deadline: 09 Jun 2026

Seasonal international art prize from Biafarin (a Canadian artist-management platform), running a juried online group exhibition with cash grants, magazine/catalogue features, ARTSY presentation, and up to 10 solo online exhibitions. CASH GRANTS (modest): Major Grant CAD 500 (1 artist); Merit Grant CAD 250 each (4 artists); Recognition Grant CAD 100 each (10 artists). Plus up to 10 Honorable Mention certificates and various promotion benefits. ELIGIBILITY: open to all visual artists worldwide aged 18+, any 2D/3D medium (painting, drawing, illustration, printmaking, photography, digital art, fiber, sculpture, mixed media); originals only, no copies/derivatives. SUBMISSION: free of charge. NOTE ON FEE: only SELECTED artists pay a fixed USD 50 selection fee (USD 40 early-bird) to participate in the exhibition; most selected artists pay this fee and do not receive a cash grant. Money-back guarantee on the selection fee until the official exhibition opening day. Up to 10 artworks per artist; max 2 may be selected. Deadline 9 June 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Belgrade Art Studio: Virtual Art Lab (12 Weeks, All Media)
item.description:
Organisation: Belgrade Art Studio

Location: Remote (online; English-language program; worldwide)

Award: PAID PROGRAM (artist pays): EUR 675 program fee. NOT a grant: no stipend, no bursary, no production budget. Includes 12 one-hour 1-on-1 mentor sessions, a final online viewing/discussion, and artist interview/promotion. No separate application fee.

Duration: 12 weeks / 3 months; weekly 1-hour individual sessions (Zoom or Google Meet); not full-time

Deadline: Rolling / undated

PAID 12-week online mentorship program from Belgrade Art Studio: each artist works one-on-one with a dedicated artist-mentor through weekly 1-hour Zoom/Google Meet sessions, aimed at artists at a turning point in their practice (stuck, seeking new directions, experimenting with unfamiliar media including AI). Hybrid seminar/workshop format supporting both physical and online presentation, plus curatorial thinking, writing, interviews, and documentation. Closes with an online viewing and moderated discussion of the works produced. ELIGIBILITY: open worldwide to artists and creatives across visual arts, music, theatre, dance, film, writing, design, photography, digital/interactive art, games, fashion, architecture, and curation. English-language program (ability to collaborate in English preferred but accommodations possible). EXPECTATIONS: artists must treat the program as they would an in-person residency (dedicated time for research/creation/reflection), submit in-progress material for social-media exposure, participate in an artist interview, and refrain from hate speech or discriminatory content. APPLY by emailing CV, summary, and portfolio (links accepted) to artlab@belgradeartstudio.com. Rolling intake (no deadline). Selected artists are announced and contacted by email. COST: EUR 675 to participate — this is a paid offering, not a funded residency.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: .ART Award 2026 (Global Art Prize)
item.description:
Organisation: .ART (with Whitewall Magazine, Chateau du Fresne, Anfitrion)

Location: Hybrid mobility (Grand Prize cash + residencies in France and Spain)

Award: Grand Prize: USD 15,000 cash. Artist Residency Award (France): one month at Chateau du Fresne, near Paris. Artist Residency Award (Spain): residency at Anfitrion in Marbella. Publication Award: editorial coverage in Whitewall Magazine (campaign media partner). .ART Domain Award: a USD 10,000 premium .ART domain name. No application fee mentioned (submission is via a .ART domain that documents the practice).

Duration: One-off open call; winners revealed during Art Basel Miami on 3 December 2026; residency prizes scheduled separately

Deadline: 01 Nov 2026

Global art prize celebrating how art is made, open to artists worldwide working in any medium or art form. Instead of a traditional application form, artists submit a digital presence (website, portfolio or social profile) linked through a .ART domain that documents identity, process and work as one continuous record. PRIZES: Grand Prize (USD 15,000 cash); Artist Residency Award France (one month at Chateau du Fresne, near Paris); Artist Residency Award Spain (residency at Anfitrion, Marbella); Publication Award (editorial coverage in Whitewall Magazine, the campaign's media partner); .ART Domain Award (USD 10,000 premium .ART domain name). ELIGIBILITY: individual artists and artist collectives from all backgrounds (cultures, ethnicities, gender identities, abilities); 18 or older at time of submission; open to artists from all geographic regions with no restrictions on nationality or place of residence. Multiple entries permitted, as long as each submission (single artwork, series or overall practice) is presented on or linked to a SEPARATE .ART domain. NOTE: a .ART domain is required for submission; this is the de facto entry mechanism. ANNOUNCEMENT: winners revealed during Art Basel Miami on 3 December 2026. DEADLINE: 1 November 2026. APPLY via the .ART Award site.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Naomi Anolic Early Career Jewish Visual Arts Award 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Anolic Family Award

Location: Online application (open to early-career Jewish artists)

Award: $1,000 award for one early-career Jewish artist. No application fee.

Duration: One-off award; project supported within the award year, with a final artist statement and visual documentation due at year end

Deadline: Closed 01 Jun 2026

Annual award providing momentum for early-career Jewish visual artists for a specific project. The 2026 cycle focuses on the fine arts: painting, drawing, sculpture and mixed media. Recipients must provide a brief artist statement and visual documentation of the supported work at year end, and must credit the Naomi Anolic Early Career Jewish Visual Arts Award in any exhibition or publication of the supported work. ELIGIBILITY: active early-career Jewish artists aged 25 to 35; students may not apply. APPLY with a max 600-word proposal (why you're applying, why you're early-career, project/focus, how the award will help, how Judaism informs and is realised in your work - overt Jewish imagery is not required), 10 jpg images (max 1024px longest side, max 4 MB each), an image list, an up-to-2-page CV/resume, and an artist website (or social-media link if no site). Award announcement by September 2026. Questions: info@anolicfamilyaward.org.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Arte Laguna Prize 2026 (21st Edition)
item.description:
Organisation: Arte Laguna (Venice)

Location: Venice, Italy (finalist exhibition at Arsenale Nord)

Award: EUR 10,000 main prize plus additional FOMAS prizes (EUR 7,000 / 4,000 / 3,000) and a finalist exhibition at the Venice Arsenale Nord. Entry fee approximately EUR 97 to EUR 122.

Duration: One-off prize

Deadline: 30 Jun 2026

International Arte Laguna Prize, open to artists across many media including dedicated digital art and video sections, with a finalist exhibition at the Venice Arsenale Nord. ELIGIBILITY: individual artists of all ages and nationalities worldwide. NOTE: an entry fee applies (roughly EUR 97 to EUR 122), but the prize awards substantial cash. Apply via the Arte Laguna Prize page.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: PhotoVogue Global Open Call 2026: Brave New Visions - Creativity as Rebellion
item.description:
Organisation: PhotoVogue (Conde Nast)

Location: Online (global)

Award: $12,000 in total grants split across three artists: a $6,000 Outstanding Vision Grant, a $4,000 Vision Grant, and a $2,000 Rising Voice Grant (emerging), plus presentation at the next PhotoVogue Festival, potential publication across Vogue's global editions, and PhotoVogue Virtual Portfolio Reviews. Free to submit.

Duration: One-off open call

Deadline: 11 Sep 2026

PhotoVogue's global open call invites photographers and video makers to use image-making as a form of rebellion - to challenge indifference, disrupt conventions and expand visual storytelling. It asks not for a theme but for a position in relation to the world. Open to photography, video and multimedia projects across all genres (fashion, documentary, portraiture, fine art, experimental). Three artists share $12,000 in grants ($6,000 Outstanding Vision, $4,000 Vision, $2,000 Rising Voice for an emerging artist), with festival presentation, potential Vogue publication and portfolio reviews. ELIGIBILITY: open to all artists worldwide aged 18+; submissions are free; previous PhotoVogue applicants may submit a new project. SUBMIT: a series of up to 15 images, or images plus video for multimedia, and/or a 60-second trailer, via Picter. IMPORTANT: AI-generated works are NOT eligible. Open 14 May to 11 September 2026 (11:59 PM CET).

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Beautiful Bizarre Art Prize 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Beautiful Bizarre Magazine

Location: Worldwide (exhibition in San Francisco, USA)

Award: Grand Prize $10,000 plus category awards (e.g. Imaginative Realism $5,000) and a dedicated Digital Art Award and Emerging Artist Award; $76,000+ total in cash and prizes. NOTE: $40 entry fee per submission.

Duration: Annual prize (exhibition follows)

Deadline: 17 Jul 2026

International, non-acquisitive art prize across seven categories including a dedicated Digital Art Award, with cash prizes and a San Francisco gallery exhibition. Grand Prize is $10,000, with category awards and over $76,000 in total cash and prizes. ELIGIBILITY: open internationally with no geographic restriction; all static media; includes an Emerging Artist Award. NOTE: there is a $40 entry fee per submission.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: FILE NOT FOUND: Open Call for Archive Residencies
item.description:
Organisation: Goethe-Institut

Location: On-site at ten cultural archives around the world; final exhibition at BLMK Cottbus, Germany

Award: EUR 4,000 (EUR 2,000 artist fee + EUR 2,000 production budget). Travel and accommodation covered up to EUR 2,000 for one person.

Duration: On-site residency of 2 weeks to 2 months, scheduled between August 2026 and May 2027

Deadline: 07 Jun 2026

Open call to participate in FILE NOT FOUND, a project exploring the risks and challenges archives face as sites of cultural memory, resistant knowledge and alternative perspectives under growing political pressure. Ten practitioners - artists, thinkers, activists, theatre makers, dancers, musicians and more - will be selected for residencies at ten cultural archives around the world. On site, residents engage with existing archival practices, explore both safe and not-so-safe spaces, investigate traditional and experimental methods of archiving, and ideally develop artistically subversive approaches that strengthen the resilience of archives against external influence. ELIGIBILITY: artists worldwide, all disciplines. Applicants review the ten participating archives and select the one they wish to apply to. The on-site residency runs 2 weeks to 2 months between August 2026 and May 2027. OUTCOME: an artwork plus a residency report, with a group exhibition at BLMK Cottbus, Germany in autumn 2027. Working language English.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).
